title: Peloton Earnings on Deck: Can Profitability Gains Revive Growth?
text: Peloton Q3 2026 Earnings Preview: Profitability Focus. Key Question: Can Peloton's focus on profitability and positive free cash flow revive investor interest and support stock growth? Profitability Metrics: The company's achievement of adjusted EBITDA of $126 million (up 41% YoY) and free cash flow of $151 million (up 59% YoY) demonstrates operational leverage. Revenue Growth: Q3 revenue of $631 million exceeded guidance, marking the inflection point back to year-over-year growth after prolonged contraction. Strategic Shift: The company's transition from growth-at-all-costs to profitability-focused operations represents a significant strategic pivot. Market Context: Investor sentiment has shifted toward profitability and cash generation, away from growth-at-any-cost, positioning Peloton's strategy favorably. Forward Outlook: Management's confidence in achieving full-year positive net income and operating income for the first time represents a major milestone. Competitive Landscape: As the home fitness market normalizes post-pandemic, Peloton's ability to maintain subscribers and expand into commercial and digital channels becomes critical.
